title: [phase:critique] [family:backend-fit] [provider_hint:codex] Review suitability of the Google AI agents technical guidance for using Google agent tooling as the backend layer for Cursorvers.Inc. Focus on risks, lock-in, security, and what should not be delegated to agents. Findings first.
summary_text: **Findings** 1. `index.ts` still owns dispatcher routing inline (tool-mode + explicit prefix), so it’s hard to test without full DB/LINE side effects ([index.ts:883](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:883), [index.ts:962](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:962)). 2. The smallest seam already exists: `routeToolCommand(...)` in `webhook-utils.ts`, with dependency injection for `handlePromptPolisher` / `handleRiskChecker` ([webhook-utils.ts:151](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/webhook-utils.ts:151)). 3. Blank-input behavior is implemented in handlers, not dispatcher; dispatcher should route empty payloads (`polish:` / `check:`) to handlers unchanged ([index.ts:676](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:676), [index.ts:760](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:760)). 4. Importing `index.ts` directly for tests is expensive/risky because of top-level `Deno.serve` ([index.ts:1137](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:1137)). **Smallest testable seam recommendation** 1. Use `routeToolCommand` as the dispatcher seam (do not create a new seam). 2. In `handleEvent`, replace duplicated routing blocks with one call to `routeToolCommand` right after existing cancel check in tool mode. 3. Add tests in `webhook-utils_test.ts` for `routeToolCommand` (it’s currently not covered; only parse helpers are) ([webhook-utils_test.ts:5](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/webhook-utils_test.ts:5)). **Minimal test cases to add** 1. `trimmed="polish:abc"` routes to `handlePromptPolisher("abc", ...)`. 2. `trimmed="check:abc"` routes to `handleRiskChecker("abc", ...)`. 3. `trimmed="polish:"` routes to `handlePromptPolisher("", ...)` (blank-input path). 4. `trimmed="check:"` routes to `handleRiskChecker("", ...)` (blank-input path). 5. `toolMode="polish"` routes to prompt handler and calls `clearUserState`. 6. `toolMode="risk_check"` routes to risk handler and calls `clearUserState`. 7. Non-command text returns `false` and calls no handler. This is the lowest-risk way to get dispatcher-level coverage for prefix routing and blank-input flow without pulling `index.ts` runtime dependencies into tests.
